The Princeton Laboratory for Artificial Intelligence supports and expands the scope of AI research at Princeton, adopting a new organizational model designed to accommodate the challenges of working in this fast-moving field.

1023.notes: The University of Cambridge is one of the world's oldest universities and leading academic centres, established in 1209. The University comprises 31 autonomous Colleges and over 100 departments, faculties and institutions. Its 25,000 students include around 11,000 international students. The University has an annual income of £2 billion.

1024.notes: The University of Oxford is described as "the oldest university in the English-speaking world" and "a world-leading centre of learning, teaching and research". Oxford has announced plans to expand its artificial intelligence capabilities through a five-year collaboration with OpenAI, providing students and faculty access to research grant funding, enterprise-level security and cutting-edge AI tools. The university is ranked number one in the world in the 2025 Times Higher Education World Rankings and is at the forefront of academic disciplines including medical sciences, mathematical and physical sciences, humanities, and social sciences. Oxford admitted 3,245 students in 2024, with approximately 80.5% of undergraduate places going to UK students.

1025.notes: UC Berkeley is a public research university founded in 1868 and headquartered in Berkeley, California. The university employs 13,995 people with an annual revenue of $2.4B and operates in 83 countries. UC Berkeley is a leading institution in artificial intelligence research, hosting multiple AI-focused research centers including the Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research (BAIR) Lab, which brings together over 50 faculty and 300+ graduate students across areas like computer vision, machine learning, natural language processing, planning, control, and robotics. The university has nearly 50 graduate programs ranked in the top 10 by U.S. News 2025 rankings, including Computer Science (#2), Engineering (#3), and Public Affairs (#3).

1026.notes: New York University is a private research university in New York City, United States, chartered in 1831 and founded in 1832 by Albert Gallatin. NYU is one of the largest private universities in the United States by enrollment, with a total of 51,848 enrolled students in 2021. The university has degree-granting campuses in New York, Abu Dhabi, and Shanghai, with 12 global academic centers. NYU is ranked among the top institutions for artificial intelligence research, placing no. 15 worldwide according to the Nature Index 2024 AI ranking.

1027.notes: The United States Senate is a legislative chamber in the bicameral legislature of the United States of America, and together with the U.S. House of Representatives makes up the U.S. Congress. First convened in 1789, the composition and powers of the Senate are established in Article One of the U.S. Constitution. Each U.S. state is represented by two senators, regardless of population, who serve staggered six-year terms. The chamber of the United States Senate is located in the north wing of the Capitol, in Washington, D.C., the national capital.

1028.notes: The U.S. House of Representatives passed a federal budget bill that includes a controversial 10-year moratorium on states regulating artificial intelligence with a vote of 215 to 214. The House established a Bipartisan Task Force on Artificial Intelligence that delivered a comprehensive report in December 2024 with 66 findings and 89 recommendations. The House held hearings on AI topics including "Advancing America's AI Action Plan" and "Building an AI-Ready America" in January 2026. The 119th Congress was sworn-in in January 2025, with Republicans holding a majority with 220 seats and Democrats holding 215 seats.

1029.notes: The California State Senate is the upper house of the California State Legislature, consisting of 40 members elected from districts apportioned on the basis of population, with one-half elected or re-elected every two years for four-year terms. The California State Senate employs 664 people and has an annual revenue of $143.0M, founded in 1849 and headquartered in Sacramento, California. In the 2024 elections, Democrats lost one seat for a 30 seat majority to Republicans' nine seats. The Senate has been actively considering multiple artificial intelligence-related bills in 2025-2026, including measures for AI safety standards, human oversight requirements, and protection of workers from AI encroachment.

1030.notes: The New York State Assembly is known as the "People's House" in New York. Since its creation in 1777, the state Assembly has helped shape history, often leading the way with progressive action and reform. Carl E. Heastie serves as Assembly Speaker, becoming New York's first African American Assembly Speaker when elected on February 3, 2015. Following the 2024 election, Democrats maintained a 103-47 majority in the Assembly.

1031.notes: The White House released its National Policy Framework for Artificial Intelligence on March 20, 2026, outlining the Trump administration's legislative priorities for federal AI governance. The framework stems from an executive order President Donald Trump signed in December that blocked states from enforcing their own regulations around artificial intelligence. The White House established the Genesis Mission in November 2025 as a national effort to accelerate the application of AI for transformative scientific discovery focused on pressing national challenges. The administration has implemented America's AI Action Plan, which recognizes the need to invest in AI-enabled science to accelerate scientific advancement.

1033.notes: The State of California has issued multiple executive orders and guidelines related to artificial intelligence under Governor Gavin Newsom, including Executive Order N-12-23 on Generative Artificial Intelligence signed on September 6, 2023. The state published GenAI Guidelines for Public Sector Procurement, Uses and Training in March 2024. Governor Newsom signed another executive order in 2026 to strengthen AI protections and responsible use while the Trump administration was rolling back federal protections. California leads the nation in AI regulations and is home to many of the largest AI companies in the world.

1034.notes: The State of Texas signed the Texas Responsible Artificial Intelligence Governance Act (TRAIGA) into law on June 22, 2025, with an effective date of January 1, 2026. The law prohibits AI systems designed to manipulate behavior causing harm, discriminate based on protected characteristics, produce child sexual abuse material, or enable social scoring by government. The Texas Attorney General has exclusive authority to enforce TRAIGA. The state's Consolidated General Revenue Fund ended fiscal 2025 with a cash balance of $41.4 billion, an increase of $4.0 billion from fiscal 2024.

1035.notes: The State of Colorado enacted Senate Bill 24-205, also known as the Colorado Anti-Discrimination in AI Law (ADAI), which was signed into law on May 17, 2024. The ADAI is part of the State of Colorado's Consumer Protection Act and its provisions go into effect February 1, 2026. The Colorado Attorney General has exclusive enforcement authority for this legislation and is tasked with creating rules to implement and enforce the ADAI's requirements. History Colorado is established as a 501(c)(3) charitable organization and an agency of the State of Colorado under the Department of Higher Education.

1036.notes: The State of New York Office of Information Technology Services established the NYS-P24-001 policy for acceptable use of AI technologies by state entities, updated on March 11, 2025. Governor Kathy Hochul announced the FY 2025 State Budget which included a $275 million investment over 10 years to support Empire AI, a consortium to secure New York's position in artificial intelligence. The New York State Executive Chamber works to meet the needs of all New Yorkers by advancing the Governor's policy initiatives and agenda, as well as overseeing government operations. The State of New York has various departments including the Department of State, which is one of the oldest and most diverse agencies in state government.

1037.notes: Ohio became the 17th state in the Union in 1803, the same year as the Louisiana Purchase. The Ohio Department of Job and Family Services received the Merrill Baumgardner Award from the Washington-based National Association of State Workforce Agencies, which honors states that demonstrate excellence in information technology and set benchmarks nationwide. Ohio has released a new policy that includes guidance and requirements for how Ohio state government workers can use AI and generative AI for work within their agencies. Governor Mike DeWine has signed Ohio's Fiscal Year 2024-2025 operating budget bill into law.

1038.notes: The Office of the Governor of Indiana is the chief executive office of the state under Article 5 of the Indiana Constitution. The current Governor is Mike Braun, who delivered his first State of the State Address on January 29, 2025. The office operates from the Indiana Statehouse in Indianapolis and coordinates directly with the Indiana Lieutenant Governor. According to company data, the Office of the Governor of Indiana employs 14 people and is classified as a Government Administration organization.

1039.notes: The Republican Party is confronting an unfamiliar fracture over artificial intelligence, with the battleground being AI regulation and the combatants being red-state lawmakers who want to protect their constituents versus a White House determined to clear the runway for Big Tech. Governors like Ron DeSantis and Spencer Cox are championing state-level AI regulations, focusing on a consumer 'Bill of Rights' and protecting children. The Trump administration has flipped the script, aggressively pressuring Republican state legislators to abandon bills designed to protect children and establish basic transparency requirements for AI companies. More than 50 Republican state lawmakers signed a letter to President Trump expressing deep concern by the work of officials seeking to pressure lawmakers in Utah and other states to abandon legislation aimed at mitigating risks.

1040.notes: The Democratic Party is the major liberal political party in the United States, sitting on the center to center-left of the political spectrum, founded in 1828, making it the world's oldest active political party. House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries announced the launch of a new House Democratic Commission on AI and the Innovation Economy that will convene throughout 2026. The Democratic National Committee (DNC) has been the heart of the Democratic Party since 1848, and is the oldest continuing political party in the United States. Ken Martin serves as the current Chairperson of the Democratic Party.

1041.notes: Google is an American multinational technology corporation focused on artificial intelligence and other technologies. The company was founded in 1998 by Larry Page and Sergey Brin. Sundar Pichai was appointed CEO of Google in 2015, replacing Larry Page, and in 2019, Pichai also became the CEO of Alphabet. Google is Alphabet's largest subsidiary and is a holding company for Alphabet's internet properties and interests.

1042.notes: Microsoft Corporation is an American multinational technology conglomerate headquartered in Redmond, Washington. Founded in 1975, the company became influential in the rise of personal computers through software like Windows, and has since expanded to Internet services, cloud computing, artificial intelligence, video gaming, and other fields. Microsoft is the largest software company by revenue, one of the most valuable public companies, and one of the most valuable brands globally. Revenue was $281.7 billion, up 15 percent in fiscal 2025.

1043.notes: Meta Platforms, Inc. is the developer of the Meta AI app and various AI products including Meta AI assistant built with Llama technology. The company develops and releases the Llama series of open-source large language models, with Llama 3 and Llama 4 being their latest offerings. Meta's mission is to build the future of human connection and the technology that makes it possible. The company reported revenue of $200.97 billion for full year 2025, representing a 22% year-over-year increase.

1044.notes: The University of Virginia is a public research university located in Charlottesville City and Albemarle County in Virginia, United States. It was founded in 1819 by Thomas Jefferson and contains his Academical Village, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. The university has been a member of the Association of American Universities since 1904. UVA's Board of Visitors approved the new LaCross Institute for Ethical Artificial Intelligence in Business at UVA's Darden School of Business.

1045.notes: Vanderbilt University was founded in 1873 by Cornelius Vanderbilt, who gave $1 million to endow and build the university. The university is a private research university with approximately 6,900 undergraduates and more than 6,200 graduate and professional students. Vanderbilt has launched the Amplify Generative AI Innovation Center within the College of Connected Computing, which is co-led by Professor Jules White and Chief AI and Technology Officer Allen Karns. The university's core purpose is to create, preserve, and disseminate knowledge through teaching, research, and the arts.

1046.notes: UCL was chosen as the sole UK partner in NVIDIA's Europe-wide initiative to help develop sovereign AI platforms, building on its strong existing relationship with NVIDIA and ongoing work creating a UK designed and trained large language model called BritLLM. UCL leads the EPSRC AI hub in Generative Models with £80 million investment, developing tools for responsible generative AI that will benefit the economy and society. UCL maintains strong partnerships with leading AI firms such as NVIDIA, Darktrace, Google DeepMind and Quantexa, as well as with the UK's AI Safety Institute. UCL is consistently ranked as one of the top ten universities in the world and is 9th in the 2026 QS World University Rankings.

1047.notes: Brown University is a leading nonprofit research university founded in 1764 and located in Providence, Rhode Island. The university has 7,272 undergraduate students, 3,130 graduate students, and 866 regular faculty. Brown received a $20 million grant from the U.S. National Science Foundation to lead the AI Research Institute on Interaction for AI Assistants (ARIA) focused on developing trustworthy AI assistants for mental and behavioral health applications. The university operates with a Fiscal Year 2025 budget of $1.79 billion in revenues and $1.83 billion in expenditures.

1048.notes: Columbia University offers a Master of Science in Artificial Intelligence program that combines core AI courses in Computer Science and Engineering with comprehensive concentrations. The university leads a $20 million NSF-funded AI Institute for Artificial and Natural Intelligence (ARNI), collaborating with academic and industry partners. Columbia operates the Center of Artificial Intelligence Technology in collaboration with Amazon, guided by the Engineering for Humanity vision. The university was founded as King's College in 1754 and renamed Columbia University in the City of New York in 1896.

1049.notes: The University of Washington is comprised of three campuses: the Seattle campus with sixteen schools and colleges, plus the Bothell and Tacoma campuses. The primary mission of the University of Washington is the preservation, advancement, and dissemination of knowledge. The UW has been recognized as the #1 most innovative public university in the world and receives more federal research dollars than any other U.S. public university. The University was founded on November 4, 1861, making it one of the oldest state-supported institutions of higher education on the Pacific coast.

1050.notes: MIT Sloan is the MIT Sloan School of Management, located in Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States. The school employs 1,398 people and operates in 49 countries. Richard M. Locke is the John C Head III Dean at MIT Sloan, beginning his appointment as dean in July 2025. MIT Sloan offers various programs including MBA, Executive Education, and specialized programs like the MIT Sloan Fellows MBA Program.

1051.notes: The Center for Humane Technology is a nonprofit organization founded in 2018 and headquartered in San Francisco, California, dedicated to radically reimagining digital infrastructure and driving a comprehensive shift toward humane technology. The organization was co-founded by Tristan Harris and Aza Raskin, with Harris previously serving as a Design Ethicist at Google where he created the viral presentation 'A Call to Minimize Distraction & Respect Users' Attention'. CHT employs 22 people and focuses on transforming the incentives that drive technology, from social media to artificial intelligence, working to catalyze systems-level change in the tech ecosystem. The organization has launched a new area of work called 'AI and What Makes Us Human' to address what new norms, legal protections, and fundamental rights are needed to preserve what makes life meaningful in the age of AI.

1052.notes: The Distributed Artificial Intelligence Research Institute (or DAIR Institute) is a research institute founded by Timnit Gebru in December 2021. The institute announced itself as "an independent, community-rooted institute set to counter Big Tech's pervasive influence on the research, development and deployment of AI". In February 2023, two other members of Google's Ethical AI research group, researcher Alex Hanna and developer Dylan Baker, left Google to join DAIR. DAIR is committed to interdisciplinary and globally distributed AI research.

1054.notes: Nonprofit developer of the Signal encrypted-messaging app. President Meredith Whittaker argues AI is fundamentally a surveillance technology that expands harmful data-collection business models. Signal has publicly warned that agentic AI poses an existential threat to secure messaging, and opposes mandatory message-scanning laws such as the EU Chat Control proposal.

1055.notes: The Gates Foundation is an American private foundation founded by Bill Gates and Melinda French Gates, based in Seattle, Washington, launched in 2000 and reported to be the third-wealthiest charitable foundation in the world, holding $86 billion in assets as of July 31, 2025. The foundation's primary stated goals are to enhance healthcare and reduce extreme poverty across the world, and to expand educational opportunities and access to information technology in the United States. In May 2025, Bill Gates announced the foundation will spend $200 billion over the next 20 years and set a new date of 2045 to sunset its operations. The foundation's 2025 budget reached $8.74 billion, which is the largest budget in its history.

1056.notes: Emerson Collective, LLC is an American company founded by Laurene Powell Jobs in 2011 in Palo Alto, California. The company invests in entrepreneurs and innovators working in education, energy and the environment, immigration, media and journalism. Emerson Collective has invested in at least nine AI startups since 2022, with involvement in AI funding rounds totaling over $1 billion. The organization operates as a limited liability company and focuses on venture capital investment and philanthropy.

1057.notes: AngelList is building the infrastructure that powers the startup economy and aims to accelerate innovation by providing startups and investors with the connected tools they need to launch and scale a startup or fund—and invest in both. AngelList reaches over $171B in assets on the platform, with over 72k investors, 13k active startups, and more than 25k funds and syndicates supported. AngelList is the parent company of AngelList Venture, AngelList Talent, and Product Hunt and has more than 20 unicorns in the portfolio. The company provides investors and innovators with the tools to grow, offering venture funds, SPVs, scout funds, and digital subscriptions.

1058.notes: Defense technology company founded in 2017 by Palmer Luckey, building AI-powered autonomous weapons, surveillance systems, and battlefield software (Lattice). Valued at $30.5B in June 2025 ($2.5B Series G led by Founders Fund); in March 2026 raising a new round led by Thrive Capital and Andreessen Horowitz targeting ~$60B. In March 2026 awarded a 10-year U.S. Army enterprise contract with a ceiling of up to $20B, consolidating 120+ separate procurement actions around Lattice — the largest single contract in Anduril's history (first task order: $87M to JIATF 401 for counter-UAS C2). Sacra estimates $2.1B revenue in 2025 (up 110% YoY), projecting $4.3B in 2026. Luckey publicly opposes corporate restrictions on military AI use, arguing elected government — not corporate executives — should set AI weapons policy.

1059.notes: The New York Times is a media organization dedicated to seeking the truth and helping people understand the world through independent journalism. The company has over 11.4 million subscribers and employs approximately 2,000 journalists in its newsroom. The New York Times has created an A.I. Initiatives team led by Editorial Director Zach Seward to leverage artificial intelligence for journalism while maintaining human oversight. The organization filed a lawsuit against Perplexity AI for unauthorized use of Times content.

1060.notes: Independent tech newsletter founded in 2020 by Casey Newton, focusing on the intersection of technology and democracy. Covers major AI labs and platforms with a critical, product-focused lens. Subscriber-funded via Ghost; Newton declined Substack advances and moved the publication off Substack in 2024 over content moderation concerns.

1061.notes: Common Sense Media is an American nonprofit organization founded by Jim Steyer in 2003 that reviews and provides ratings for media and technology with the goal of providing information on their suitability for children. The organization is headquartered in San Francisco, California and is dedicated to improving the lives of kids and families by providing trustworthy information, education, and independent voice they need to thrive. Common Sense Media rates movies, TV shows, books, and more so parents can feel good about the entertainment choices they make for their kids, and offers the largest, most trusted library of independent age-based ratings and reviews. The organization also advocates publicly for child-friendly policies and laws regarding media and education, and funds research on the role of media in the lives of children.

1062.notes: Research initiative co-founded by Eli Pariser and Talia Stroud, originally a project of the National Conference on Citizenship and the Center for Media Engagement at UT Austin. Developed a framework of 14 design principles ("signals") for evaluating and building healthier digital public spaces, based on a 20-country YouGov survey of 20,000+ people funded by the Omidyar Network. Rebranded as New_Public in 2021 and continues work on digital public infrastructure. Advocates for treating digital spaces like physical public infrastructure (libraries, parks, town squares) with civic design principles. Jennifer Pahlka is affiliated (edge: employer).

1063.notes: The Amazon Labor Union (ALU) was founded by warehouse workers at the JFK8 Fulfillment Center in Staten Island, New York, and is now known as Amazon Labor Union–IBT Local 1. The NLRB recently ordered Amazon.com Services to bargain with Amazon Labor Union at a fulfillment center located in Staten Island, N.Y. (JFK8 Facility). Chris Smalls is identified as the Amazon Labor Union founder who wrote a book called 'When the Revolution Comes'. The union operates as a worker-led union committed to transforming conditions at Amazon and describes itself as a fighting union built by and for Amazon workers.

1064.notes: The National Bureau of Economic Research (NBER) is a private, nonprofit organization that facilitates cutting-edge research on and analysis of major economic issues, founded in New York City in 1920 and today headquartered in Cambridge, Massachusetts. The NBER is a network of more than 1,800 academic economists, with 43 current or former NBER affiliates having been awarded the Nobel Prize in Economic Sciences, and 13 have chaired the President's Council of Economic Advisers. The organization recently conducted a study surveying nearly 6,000 top executives across the US, UK, Germany and Australia about AI's impact on employment and productivity. The current president and chief executive officer is MIT professor of economics James Poterba.

1065.notes: Google Brain was a deep learning artificial intelligence research team that served as the sole AI branch of Google before being incorporated under the newer umbrella of Google AI, a research division at Google dedicated to artificial intelligence. Formed in 2011, it combined open-ended machine learning research with information systems and large-scale computing resources. It was merged into former Google sister company DeepMind to form Google DeepMind in April 2023. Google Brain was initially established by Google Fellow Jeff Dean and visiting Stanford professor Andrew Ng.

1066.notes: Google Research is the research division of Google, a subsidiary of Alphabet Inc. According to its official website, Google Research publishes findings, releases open-source software, and applies research results within Google products and services as well as within the wider scientific community. Google Research maintains a portfolio of research projects driven by fundamental research, new product innovation, product contribution and infrastructure goals. The organization is led by Yossi Matias, Vice President & Head of Google Research.

1067.notes: The Council on Foreign Relations (CFR) is a nonpartisan, independent national membership organization, think tank, educator, and publisher founded in 1921. CFR publishes Foreign Affairs magazine and generates policy-relevant ideas and analysis on the most consequential issues facing the United States and the world. The organization has 1,277 employees and an annual revenue of $77.3 million. CFR's mission is to inform U.S. engagement with the world and convenes experts and policymakers to promote informed public discussion.

1068.notes: The World Economic Forum is an independent, not-for-profit organization, headquartered in Geneva, Switzerland, with offices in New York and San Francisco, United States, Beijing, China, and Tokyo, Japan. Founded in 1971 as a not-for-profit foundation, the World Economic Forum set out with a transformative point of view: that business should not only to create economic value but also to serve society and the planet. The Forum is the International Organization for Public-Private Cooperation that engages the foremost political, business and other leaders of society to shape global, regional and industry agendas. The Forum has served as an impartial, globally respected platform for cooperation for more than five decades.

1069.notes: Microsoft Research is a multidisciplinary global organization working to create scientific knowledge and deliver innovation that leads to positive human impact at scale. For nearly 35 years, Microsoft Research has shaped the technology people use every day through a commitment to hiring the world's top minds and giving them autonomy, resources, and support to conduct research. Microsoft Research has research labs across the globe and mission-focused efforts in areas like scientific discovery and human health, conducting fundamental science and technology research that benefits humanity. The organization was founded in 1991, with the original Microsoft Research Plan memo serving as the catalyst for the development of the organization.

1070.notes: Facebook AI Research (FAIR) Paris was created in 2015 to advance the field of artificial intelligence through open research for the benefit of all. FAIR has grown into an international research organization with labs in Menlo Park, New York, Paris, Montreal, Tel Aviv, Seattle, Pittsburgh, and London. For more than a decade, Meta's Fundamental AI Research (FAIR) team has focused on advancing the state of the art in AI through open research. FAIR has applied an open model to all aspects of their work, collaborating broadly with the community by publishing cutting-edge research early and often, and open-sourcing research code, data sets, and tools like PyTorch, fastText, FAISS, and Detectron.

1071.notes: Tesla announced a joint project with xAI called Digital Optimus, also nicknamed 'Macrohard,' designed to automate complex office workflows by observing and replicating human interactions with computers. As part of this collaboration, Tesla made a $2 billion investment in xAI in 2026. Tesla's financial performance declined significantly in 2025, with profits falling 46 percent and revenue dropping for the first time in the company's history. The company's automotive revenues fell by 11 percent to $17.7 billion in Q4 2025, though energy storage business grew 25 percent.

1072.notes: Stripe was founded in 2010 by two Irish brothers, Patrick Collison (CEO) and John Collison (President), who came to the US in 2006 at ages 18 and 16 respectively. In 2025, businesses running on Stripe generated $1.9 trillion in total volume, up 34% from 2024, and equivalent to roughly 1.6% of global GDP. Stripe now powers more than 5 million businesses directly or via platforms, including 90% of the Dow Jones Industrial Average and 80% of the Nasdaq 100. The company has developed AI-focused products including the Stripe integration benchmark for evaluating AI agents' ability to build complete Stripe integrations, and the Agentic Commerce Suite for enabling businesses to sell on AI agents.

1073.notes: Palantir Technologies is a data analytics and AI company that builds software platforms for data integration and analysis, serving government and commercial clients. The company was founded in 2003 by Peter Thiel and others, with Alex Karp joining as CEO in 2004. Palantir's Maven AI system has been adopted by the Pentagon as a core military system for weapons targeting and battlefield data analysis. The company reported Q4 2025 revenue of $1.41 billion, up 70% year-over-year, with a market capitalization of approximately $100.6 billion.

1074.notes: DeepLearning.AI was founded in 2017 by Andrew Ng, a global leader in AI. The company is an education technology company that empowers the global workforce to build an AI-powered future through world-class education, hands-on training, and a collaborative community. DeepLearning.AI is making a world-class AI education accessible to people around the globe. The company is headquartered in Mountain View, California and employs 237 people.

1075.notes: Coursera was founded by Stanford University professors Andrew Ng and Daphne Koller in 2012. The company has grown to serve 197 million registered learners as of December 31, 2025. Coursera partners with over 375 leading university and industry partners to offer courses, specializations, professional certificates, and degrees. The company went public on the New York Stock Exchange in March 2021.

1076.notes: BlackRock is a global asset manager and technology provider with over $14 trillion in assets under management as of Q4 2025. The company has formed an AI Infrastructure Partnership with Microsoft, raising $12.5 billion toward a $30 billion goal. BlackRock has built an AI research platform dubbed 'Asimov' for use in its fundamental equity business. The company's CEO Larry Fink has framed AI as 'the most significant technology since, at least, the computer' in his 2026 annual letter to investors.

1077.notes: The University of Toronto is described as a world-leader in Artificial Intelligence (AI) research, including machine and deep learning, natural language processing, and computer vision. The university has established the Hinton Chair in Artificial Intelligence with $10 million in funding from Google, with U of T matching Google's support with an additional $10 million in funding. U of T ranked 21st globally in the Times Higher Education World University Rankings 2025 and is the top-ranked university in Canada. The university was founded in 1827 and has three campuses: St. George, Mississauga, and Scarborough.

1078.notes: Carnegie Mellon University was founded in 1900 by philanthropist Andrew Carnegie who donated $1 million to establish a school where students from all backgrounds could gain skills to uplift their careers and communities. The university was originally called Carnegie Technical Schools (1900-1912), then Carnegie Institute of Technology (1912-1967), and became Carnegie Mellon University in 1967 when Carnegie Tech merged with the Mellon Institute. Carnegie Mellon is described as the birthplace of AI over half a century ago and maintains the world's #1 ranked AI program.

1079.notes: Georgetown University is a Catholic and Jesuit, student-centered research university established in 1789. The university offers an online Master of Professional Studies in Artificial Intelligence Management through Georgetown SCS, which is a 30-credit program with tuition of $52,560. Georgetown has adopted Google Gemini for students, faculty and staff as part of its AI initiatives. The university has more than 22,000 undergraduate and graduate students who take classes at five locations: Main Campus, Medical Center, Law Center, School of Continuing Studies, and the university's Georgetown Qatar campus.

1080.notes: Yale University was founded in 1701 and is committed to improving the world today and for future generations through outstanding research and scholarship, education, preservation, and practice. Yale announced over $150 million in funding over five years to support faculty, students, and staff as they engage with artificial intelligence. The university is located in New Haven, Connecticut and has approximately 15,564 total students including 6,814 undergraduates and 8,750 graduate students. Yale's mission includes educating aspiring leaders worldwide who serve all sectors of society through the free exchange of ideas in an ethical, interdependent, and diverse community.

1081.notes: The United Nations is an international organization founded in 1945, currently made up of 193 Member States, with work guided by the purposes and principles contained in its founding Charter. The UN has established a High-Level Advisory Body on AI to analyze the current situation and recommend governance approaches for artificial intelligence. The General Assembly approved a $3.72 billion budget for the United Nations for 2025. The UN Charter was signed on 26 June 1945 in San Francisco and came into force on 24 October, which is now recognized as UN Day.

1082.notes: The European Union is a political and economic union of 27 member states that are located primarily in Europe. The EU was established, along with its citizenship, when the Maastricht Treaty came into force in 1993, and was incorporated as an international legal juridical person upon entry into force of the Treaty of Lisbon in 2009. In 2012, the EU was awarded the Nobel Peace Prize. The European Union has enacted the Artificial Intelligence Act, which is a European Union regulation concerning artificial intelligence that entered into force on 1 August 2024.

1083.notes: DARPA is a research and development agency of the United States Department of Defense responsible for the development of emerging technologies for use by the military. The agency was created on February 7, 1958, by President Dwight D. Eisenhower in response to the Soviet's launch of Sputnik 1 in 1957. DARPA comprises about 220 government employees in six technical offices, including nearly 100 program managers. The agency's annual budget is $4.122 billion (FY2024).

1084.notes: The University of Michigan is a public research university in Ann Arbor, Michigan, United States, founded in 1817 and is the oldest institution of higher education in the state. The university reported record total enrollment of 52,855 students for fall 2024, with undergraduate enrollment of 34,454 students. U-M has annual research volume that exceeds $1.86 billion and employs 20,482 people. The university received a record number of applications for fall 2025 with more than 115,000 prospective first-year and transfer students.

1085.notes: UCLA has an AI Innovation Initiative that is a campus-wide effort to build AI fluency, foster innovation, and drive responsible AI adoption. The initiative includes strategic partnerships with leaders like OpenAI and Google to empower faculty, staff, and students. UCLA's primary purpose as a public research university is the creation, dissemination, preservation and application of knowledge for the betterment of our global society. The university operates on a quarter system with Fall, Winter, Spring quarters and Summer sessions.

1086.notes: Johns Hopkins University offers multiple artificial intelligence programs including an online Master of Science in Artificial Intelligence developed with expertise from the Johns Hopkins Applied Physics Lab. The university also has a Johns Hopkins Data Science and AI Institute that focuses on research and education in data science, machine learning, and artificial intelligence across diverse fields. The university's mission is to educate students and cultivate their capacity for lifelong learning, to foster independent and original research, and to bring the benefits of discovery to the world. Johns Hopkins University was founded in 1876 with the inauguration of its first president, Daniel Coit Gilman.

1087.notes: The University of Louisville offers multiple artificial intelligence programs including a Graduate Certificate in Artificial Intelligence in Medicine and a Master of Science in Artificial Intelligence in Medicine. The university was established in 1798 and is a state supported research university located in Kentucky's largest metropolitan area. As of Fall 2024, the university has a total student enrollment of 24,123 with faculty and staff totaling 7,231. The university operates with an annual revenue of $1.3 billion and has three campuses including the 287-acre Belknap Campus.

1088.notes: American University is a private federally chartered research university in Washington, D.C., chartered by Congress in 1893. The university consists of eight schools and colleges, including the Kogod School of Business, School of International Service, College of Arts and Sciences, School of Communication, School of Professional and Extended Studies, School of Public Affairs, Linda A. and H. Kent Baker School of Education, and Washington College of Law. American University has 13,019 students as of fall 2023 and offers over 160 academic programs. The Kogod School of Business has launched the Institute for Applied Artificial Intelligence (IAAI) and has been recognized for having 'the most consequential AI transformation in business education' according to Poets&Quants.

1089.notes: SUNY Albany is also known as University at Albany, UAlbany, or SUNY Albany and is a public research university in Albany, New York, United States. The university was founded in 1844 and is one of four "university centers" of the State University of New York (SUNY) system. In 2024, the university enrolled 17,567 students in nine schools and colleges, which offer over 50 undergraduate majors and over 150 graduate programs. The university is classified among "R1: Doctoral Universities – Very high research spending and doctorate production".

1090.notes: USC is a private university founded in 1880 that is home to more than 48,000 students and over 4,400 full-time faculty. USC launched the Frontiers of Computing initiative in 2023, a $1B-plus initiative for advanced computation, quantum computing, AI and ethics as the largest, most comprehensive academic initiative in USC's history. USC has multiple AI research centers and initiatives, including the USC Center for AI in Society founded in 2016, which is a collaboration between USC's Suzanne Dworak-Peck School of Social Work and Viterbi School of Engineering. The university provides every student, faculty member, staff member and researcher with secure, enterprise-grade AI tools.

1091.notes: The Wharton School of the University of Pennsylvania was founded in 1881 as the world's first collegiate business school. It has more than 235 renowned professors and 5,000 undergraduate, MBA, executive MBA, and doctoral students. In April 2025, Wharton announced a new undergraduate concentration and MBA major in Artificial Intelligence for Business. The school is led by Dean Erika James.

1092.notes: Tulane University is a private research university in New Orleans, Louisiana, United States that was founded as the Medical College of Louisiana in 1834 by a cohort of medical doctors. The institution became private under the endowments of Paul Tulane and Josephine Louise Newcomb in 1884 and 1887. Tulane has been a member of the Association of American Universities since 1958 and is classified among "R1: Doctoral Universities – Very high research activity". The university has established multiple AI-related initiatives including the Connolly Alexander Institute for Data Science, the Jurist Center for Artificial Intelligence, and the Center for Community-Engaged Artificial Intelligence.

1093.notes: The U.S. Department of Transportation (DOT) is committed to safety and innovation and sees artificial intelligence (AI) as a promising capability to help achieve these aims. The Department has established an AI Compliance Plan in response to the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) Memorandum M-25-21, Accelerating Federal Use of AI through Innovation, Governance, and Public Trust. The Department was authorized in October 1966 and launched on 1 April 1967, with a mission to ensure that federal funds were effectively used to support the national transportation program. The mission of the USDOT is to ensure our nation has the safest, most efficient and modern transportation system in the world; that improves the quality of life for all American people and communities, from rural to urban, and increases the productivity and competitiveness of American workers and businesses.

1094.notes: Peter Norvig is a Distinguished Education Fellow at Stanford's Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence Institute and a researcher at Google Inc. He co-authored "Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach" with Stuart Russell, which is the leading textbook in the field used in more than 1,500 universities in 135 countries. He was born on December 14, 1956, and previously served as head of NASA Ames's Computational Sciences Division where he was NASA's senior computer scientist. He co-taught an online Artificial Intelligence class that signed up 160,000 students, helping to kick off massive open online classes.

1096.notes: The University of California, San Francisco (UCSF or UC San Francisco) is a public land-grant research university in San Francisco, California, United States that is part of the University of California system and is dedicated entirely to health science and life science. UCSF was founded as Toland Medical College in 1864, became affiliated with the University of California as its medical department in 1873, gained full administrative independence as a campus of the UC system in 1964, and gained its current name in 1970. UCSF's mission is to advance health worldwide through preeminent biomedical research, graduate-level education in the life sciences and health professions, and excellence in patient care. In 2023, UCSF received the 2nd highest research funding from the National Institutes of Health.

1097.notes: Bayesian Logic Inc. is co-founded by Nimar Arora, who serves as Chief Executive Officer, Secretary, Chief Financial Officer, and Director. Stuart Russell serves as a Director and is described as founder and Vice-President of the company. The company was officially filed as a General Corporation in California on October 24, 2011, and maintains active status. Bayesian Logic Inc. developed NET-VISA, a Bayesian seismic monitoring system for detecting nuclear explosions, which has been deployed by the CTBT organization of the U.N. and achieves around 60% reduction in missed events compared to currently deployed systems.

1098.notes: Michael Genesereth (born 1948) is an American logician and computer scientist, who is most known for his work on computational logic and applications of that work in enterprise management, computational law, and general game playing. Genesereth is professor in the Computer Science Department at Stanford University and a professor by courtesy in the Stanford Law School. His 1987 textbook on Logical Foundations of Artificial Intelligence remains one of the key references on symbolic artificial intelligence. He is the author of the influential Game Description Language(GDL) and Knowledge Interchange Format(KIF), the latter of which led to the ISO Common Logic standard.

1099.notes: Tom Cotton is a U.S. Senator representing Arkansas who introduced the DATA Act of 2026, which would allow AI data center companies to bypass federal electricity regulations by building independent energy infrastructure. Cotton stated that "American dominance in artificial intelligence and other crucial emerging industries should not come at the expense of Arkansans paying higher energy costs". On November 13, 2024, Senate Republicans selected Cotton as the next conference chair, making him the third-highest ranked Republican in the Senate. Cotton previously co-introduced a resolution with Senator Chris Coons to affirm America's artificial intelligence dominance.

1100.notes: Andy Kim is a U.S. Senator from New Jersey who serves as a Democrat on multiple committees including Commerce, Science, and Transportation. He has been actively involved in AI-related legislation, co-introducing the Artificial Intelligence Ready Data Act with Senator Ted Budd to open government data sets for training American AI models. Kim also co-introduced the AI Talent Act with Senator Jon Husted to create an AI and tech talent team within the U.S. Office of Personnel Management. He was born on July 12, 1982, in Boston, Massachusetts, to South Korean immigrant parents and is the first Korean American elected to the U.S. Senate.

1101.notes: Jim Risch is a U.S. Senator from Idaho who has served since 2009 and is a member of the Republican Party. He serves on five key committees: Energy and Natural Resources, Foreign Relations, Small Business and Entrepreneurship, Intelligence, and Ethics, and serves as Chairman of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee. He previously served as Idaho's 31st Governor in 2006 and as Lieutenant Governor, and achieved major tax reform as Governor, cutting property taxes by 20%. He is consistently recognized as one of the most conservative members of the U.S. Senate.

1102.notes: Ben Horowitz is a cofounder and general partner at the venture capital firm Andreessen Horowitz. He previously co-founded and served as president and chief executive officer of the enterprise software company Opsware, which Hewlett-Packard acquired in 2007. Andreessen Horowitz recently raised more than $15 billion across five new funds, with Horowitz stating that at this moment of profound technological opportunity, it is fundamentally important for humanity that America wins. He has authored books including The Hard Thing About Hard Things: Building a Business When There Are No Easy Answers.

1103.notes: Donald Trump served as the 45th President of the United States from 2017 to 2021 and was inaugurated as the 47th president on January 20, 2025. On March 20, 2026, the Trump Administration released a National AI Legislative Framework that addresses six key objectives including protecting children, safeguarding American communities, and ensuring American AI dominance. In December 2025, President Trump signed an Executive Order to protect American AI innovation from inconsistent state laws and directed the Attorney General to establish an AI Litigation Task Force. Trump has appointed David Sacks as White House AI 'Czar' who has called the administration's AI policy the '5 Cs': child safety, communities, creators, censorship, and competitiveness.

1104.notes: Coinbase is joining the x402 Foundation alongside tech titans like Amazon, Google, and Stripe. Coinbase has launched crypto wallet infrastructure that allows AI agents to spend, earn and trade crypto. The company's mission is to increase economic freedom for more than 1 billion people by providing a trusted platform for crypto assets. Coinbase delivered strong Q4 and full year 2024 results, with full year revenue of $6.6 billion, net income of $2.6 billion, and Adjusted EBITDA of $3.3 billion.

1105.notes: Sen. John Kennedy (R-LA) questioned David Evan Harris about AI regulation during a Senate Judiciary Committee hearing on September 17, 2024. Kennedy co-introduced the AI Labeling Act (S. 2691) with Sen. Brian Schatz (D-HI) on October 24, 2023, which would notify consumers when they interact with AI-generated content and require developers to disclose such content. Kennedy has introduced legislation to withhold senators' pay during government shutdowns, with the U.S. Senate Rules Committee unanimously advancing this proposal in December 2025. Kennedy reacted positively to President Trump's joint address to Congress in March 2025, stating "President Trump has given us a blueprint".

1106.notes: Cory Gardner is President & CEO of NCTA – The Internet & Television Association, beginning his tenure on September 22, 2025. He previously served as a U.S. Senator from Colorado from 2015 to 2021 and as a U.S. Representative for Colorado's 4th Congressional District from 2011 to 2015. Gardner served as chair of the National Republican Senatorial Committee from 2017 to 2019. He was consistently ranked among the most bipartisan senators, including being ranked the 5th most bipartisan senator of the 115th Congress in March 2019.

1107.notes: The Senate Commerce, Science, and Transportation Committee is a standing committee of the United States Senate composed of 28 Senators and led by Committee Chairman Ted Cruz (R-Texas) and Ranking Member Maria Cantwell (D-Wash.). The committee has broad jurisdiction over all matters concerning interstate commerce, science and technology policy, and transportation, and is one of the largest of the Senate's standing committees. The committee has six subcommittees which together oversee issues ranging from communications, highways, aviation, rail, shipping, transportation security, merchant marine, the Coast Guard, oceans, fisheries, weather, disasters, science, space, interstate commerce, tourism, consumer issues, economic development, technology, competitiveness, product safety, and insurance. The committee passed the bipartisan Future of AI Innovation Act to maintain United States leadership in the global race to develop artificial intelligence (AI) and other emerging technologies.

1108.notes: The Special Competitive Studies Project (SCSP) is a bipartisan, non-profit initiative with a mission to make recommendations to strengthen America's long-term competitiveness as artificial intelligence (AI) and other emerging technologies reshape national security, economy, and society. SCSP carries forward the vital mission of the National Security Commission on Artificial Intelligence (NSCAI), which was established in 2018. The organization was founded in 2021 and has 98 employees, located in Crystal City, United States. SCSP has launched multiple commissions and task forces, including collaborations with NVIDIA on AI and the future of work, and commissions on fusion energy and robotics for advanced manufacturing.

1109.notes: The Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research (BAIR) Lab brings together UC Berkeley researchers across the areas of computer vision, machine learning, natural language processing, planning, and robotics. BAIR includes over two dozen faculty and more than a hundred graduate students pursuing research on fundamental advances in the above areas as well as cross-cutting themes including multi-modal deep learning, human-compatible AI, and connecting AI with other scientific disciplines and the humanities. The lab was established in 1990 as part of the University of California, Berkeley. BAIR operates the Open Research Commons (ORC) which brings together academia and industry to cultivate an open, interdisciplinary research environment.

1110.notes: The Berkeley Institute for Data Science (BIDS) is a central hub of data-intensive research, open source software, and data science training programs at UC Berkeley that launched in 2013. BIDS is an integral part of UC Berkeley's College of Computing, Data Science, and Society (CDSS) and was made possible by grants from the Gordon and Betty Moore Foundation and the Alfred P. Sloan Foundation. The Institute is one of three Moore-Sloan Data Science Environments along with data science organizations at the University of Washington and New York University. BIDS is currently led by Fernando Pérez as Faculty Director.

1111.notes: Multiple organizations operate under the name "Responsible AI Initiative" with different scopes and affiliations. The University of Utah operates the One-U Responsible AI Initiative, which aims to advance AI to achieve societal good while protecting privacy, civil rights, and civil liberties. Emory University launched a Responsible AI website as part of their enterprise-wide Responsible AI initiative, led by the Emory AI Group with input from the Responsible AI Executive Governance Committee. Carnegie Mellon University launched a Responsible AI initiative out of the Block Center for Technology and Society with support from faculty in the School of Computer Science.

1112.notes: Université de Montréal is a French-language institution of higher learning founded in 1878, regarded as one of the most important universities in Canada in terms of research and one of the largest by enrolment. The university has 16 faculties and two affiliated schools (Polytechnique Montréal and HEC Montréal), with an enrollment of nearly 67,600 students in over 600 programs. UdeM ranked 9th globally in Artificial Intelligence research according to the Shanghai Academic Ranking of World Universities in 2026. The university has an annual revenue of $212.0M and employs 7,578 people.

1113.notes: Mila - Quebec Artificial Intelligence Institute is the world's largest academic AI research center with over 1,400 researchers and 140+ professors. Founded in 1993 by Yoshua Bengio at Université de Montréal, it is an independent nonprofit research organization dedicated to advancing artificial intelligence through fundamental research and talent development. The institute is recognized worldwide for its major contributions to AI, particularly in deep learning, language modeling, machine translation, object recognition and generative models. Mila is part of the Pan-Canadian Artificial Intelligence Strategy alongside Alberta's Amii and Toronto's Vector Institute.

1114.notes: Element AI was a Canadian multinational artificial intelligence company based in Montreal, Quebec that was founded in October 2016. The company was funded by the Government of Canada for CAD$5 million, and raised US$102 million independently, before being acquired by ServiceNow. ServiceNow announced it has signed an agreement to acquire Element AI on November 30, 2020, with ServiceNow completing the acquisition of Element AI on January 8, 2021. The company was running out of money and options and sold for US $230 million.

1115.notes: Ian J. Goodfellow (born 1987) is an American computer scientist, engineer, and executive, most noted for his work on artificial neural networks and deep learning. He is a research scientist at Google DeepMind, was previously employed as a research scientist at Google Brain and director of machine learning at Apple as well as one of the first employees at OpenAI. He is best known for inventing generative adversarial networks (GANs), using deep learning to generate images. He co-wrote, as the first author, the textbook Deep Learning (2016) and wrote the chapter on deep learning in the authoritative textbook of the field of artificial intelligence, Artificial Intelligence: A Modern Approach.

1116.notes: McGill University was founded in 1821 from a generous bequest by James McGill, a prominent Scottish merchant. The university offers an Applied Artificial Intelligence Minor in the Faculty of Engineering that provides foundation for applying AI techniques in various fields. McGill has several AI-related organizations including the McGill Centre for Intelligent Machines, which has been a world leader in research on intelligent systems since 1985. The McGill Collaborative for AI and Society (McCAIS) aims to understand the impact of AI on society and develop evidence-based best practices for its responsible use.

1117.notes: The International Association for Safe and Ethical AI (IASEAI) is a non-profit organization founded in 2024 with headquarters in Paris, France. The organization's stated mission is to address the risks and opportunities associated with advances in artificial intelligence and to ensure that AI systems operate safely and ethically, benefiting all of humanity. IASEAI held its inaugural conference, IASEAI '25, on February 6–7, 2025 in Paris, which brought together experts from academia, civil society, industry, and government to discuss developments in AI safety and ethics. The organization is led by Stuart Russell as President and Mark Nitzberg as Interim Executive Director.

1118.notes: The National Republican Senatorial Committee (NRSC) is the Republican Hill committee for the United States Senate, working to elect Republicans to the Senate. The NRSC was founded in 1916 as the Republican Senatorial Campaign Committee and was reorganized in 1948 and renamed the National Republican Senatorial Committee. It has been chaired by Senator Tim Scott from South Carolina since 2025. The NRSC raised $88 million in 2025, which is the best off-year fundraising for the Committee since 2021.

1119.notes: Katie Britt is a U.S. Senator from Alabama serving since 2023, and is a member of the Republican Party. She is calling for guardrails on artificial intelligence chatbots because of the potential to harm minors. Britt has two teenagers, ages 15 and 16, and draws on her experience as a mother in her advocacy work. She previously served as president and CEO of the Business Council of Alabama from 2019 to 2021, and as chief of staff for Senator Richard Shelby from 2016.

1120.notes: Correspondent at TIME magazine based in London, covering the tech industry with a focus on AI and social media. Known for investigative reporting on AI labor exploitation: broke the story that OpenAI used outsourced Kenyan workers earning less than $2/hour to detoxify ChatGPT. Also reported that OpenAI lobbied to water down the EU AI Act. His investigation "Inside Facebook's African Sweatshop" was a finalist for the 2022 Orwell Prize. Reporting focuses on how tech companies restructure power, with attention to impacts on marginalized communities, workers, and postcolonial states.

1123.notes: CEO of Salesforce and owner of TIME magazine. Major tech industry figure who has been increasingly vocal about AI regulation. At Davos 2026, called AI models "suicide coaches" and demanded reform of Section 230 to hold AI companies accountable for harms. Framed AI safety as a values question: "What's more important to us, growth or our kids?" Salesforce has launched Agentforce (enterprise AI agents) while Benioff simultaneously pushes for guardrails. Consistent pattern of calling for tech regulation — made similar calls about social media at Davos 2018. Advocates for responsible AI deployment with meaningful safety requirements.

1127.notes: One of the oldest AI research labs, founded at Stanford in 1962 by John McCarthy. Research spans NLP, computer vision, robotics, reinforcement learning, and computational biomedicine. In 2025, Carlos Guestrin became Director and SAIL formally joined forces with Stanford HAI, integrating safety and policy work alongside core capabilities research.

1128.notes: Stanford University research center founded in 2018 by Clark Barrett, David Dill, Mykel Kochenderfer, and Dorsa Sadigh, operating as an industrial affiliates program. Conducts research in formal verification, robust decision-making, and trustworthy AI; distinct from the independent nonprofit Center for AI Safety (safe.ai) run by Dan Hendrycks. Focus spans technical safety, fairness, accountability, and policy.

1130.notes: MIT research group within the Laboratory for Information and Decision Systems (LIDS), founded in 2015 by Principal Research Scientist Kalyan Veeramachaneni. Develops large-scale AI and ML systems that work alongside humans in applications spanning healthcare, finance, education, and energy. Notable for the Synthetic Data Vault open-source project, spun out commercially as DataCebo.

1131.notes: Joint partnership between the Department of the Air Force (DAF) and MIT to advance AI research for defense and civilian applications. Established 2019; renewed for another five years in 2024. Combines military personnel (embedded officers and enlisted Airmen) with MIT faculty, researchers, and students. Has launched 20+ projects engaging 150+ researchers across MIT campus and MIT Lincoln Laboratory. Focus areas include rapid prototyping, scaling, and ethical application of AI algorithms for national security. Leverages universities, small businesses, traditional defense contractors, and non-traditional commercial ventures.

1132.notes: MIT's cross-disciplinary computing college founded in 2018 with a $1B endowment anchored by a $350M gift from Stephen Schwarzman. Led by founding dean Daniel Huttenlocher, it integrates AI research across all MIT departments. Hosts the MIT AI Policy Forum and publishes governance-focused policy briefs; advocates for technically-informed, sector-specific AI regulation.

1133.notes: Harvard University-wide institute for the study of natural and artificial intelligence, founded 2022 with $500M gift from Mark Zuckerberg and Priscilla Chan (Chan Zuckerberg Initiative). Co-directed by Sham Kakade (CS) and Bernardo Sabatini (neurobiology). Operates the Kempner AI Cluster at MGHPCC (1,144 H100 GPUs as of March 2026, ranked 85th on TOP500 in 2024). Research spans mathematical models of intelligence, cognitive theories, neurobiological basis of intelligence, and AI applications in science. Supports undergraduate research (KURE program), postdocs, and 10 new faculty lines. Sits at the intersection of neuroscience and AI, seeking fundamental principles underlying both human and machine intelligence.

1134.notes: Intercollegiate high-performance computing facility in Holyoke, Massachusetts, jointly operated by Boston University, Harvard, MIT, Northeastern, UMass, and Yale. LEED Platinum certified; powered by Holyoke Gas & Electric with >90% carbon-free energy (>70% local hydro and solar). Hosts hundreds of thousands of CPUs, millions of GPU cores, >50PB storage. Announced $16M expansion in Oct 2024 to build a quantum computing complex. Houses the Kempner AI Cluster for Harvard. Connected to Holyoke's municipal fiber grid and numerous campus, regional, and national networks.

1135.notes: Charles C. Fitzmorris Professor of Computer Science at Princeton and founding director of Princeton Language and Intelligence (PLI). Indian-American theoretical computer scientist (PhD UC Berkeley, joined Princeton 1994). Research focuses on mathematical understanding of deep learning — optimization, generalization, interpretability — to enable better and safer AI. Member of the National Academy of Sciences and American Academy of Arts and Sciences. Awards include ACM Prize in Computing (2011), Fulkerson Prize (2012), Gödel Prize (2001, 2010), Packard Fellowship (1997), ACM Doctoral Dissertation Award (1995). Fellow of the ACM.

1136.notes: Class of 1987 Professor of Politics at Princeton and Director of the Center for Statistics and Machine Learning. Previously on faculty at Harvard and NYU. PhD University of Rochester; BA/MA London School of Economics. Research centers on quantitative methods for political behavior, text-as-data, and problems at the intersection of data science and social science. Has published on LLMs in politics (e.g. 'Large Language Models Can Argue in Convincing Ways About Politics, But Humans Dislike AI Authors'), the ethics of using proprietary LLMs in academic research (Nature Computational Science 2023), and advocacy for open generative AI models in science (Nature 2023).

1137.notes: Maduraperuma/Khot Professor of Computer Science at Princeton; director of Princeton Precision Health. Also deputy director of Genomics at the Flatiron Institute (Simons Foundation) since 2014. PhD in Biomedical Informatics from Stanford. Research builds AI methods to decode genomes and diagnose/treat complex diseases, focusing on the non-coding genome and multi-omics modeling. Affiliated with Lewis-Sigler Institute for Integrative Genomics, Dept of Molecular Biology, and Omenn-Darling Bioengineering Institute. Awards: MIT Technology Review Top Young Innovator, Sloan Research Fellowship, NSF CAREER award, ISCB Overton Award, Genetics Society of America Herskowitz Award.

1138.notes: Henry R. Luce Professor of Information Technology, Consciousness, and Culture at Princeton, jointly appointed in Psychology and Computer Science. Director of the Computational Cognitive Science Lab (CoCoSci) and inaugural director of the Princeton Laboratory for Artificial Intelligence (AI Lab). PhD Psychology from Stanford (2005); previously at Brown and UC Berkeley (full professor 2015, director of Berkeley's Institute of Cognitive and Brain Sciences). Research applies mathematical modeling and computational methods to understand human cognition, and uses psychological concepts to interpret AI systems. Co-authored 'Algorithms to Live By' (2016) with Brian Christian.

1139.notes: Princeton's AI Lab supports and expands AI research across the university with a novel organizational model. Directed by Tom Griffiths; associate director Olga Russakovsky. Supports three research initiatives: Princeton Language and Intelligence (large AI models, led by Sanjeev Arora), AI for Accelerating Invention (AI^2, AI in science/engineering), and Natural and Artificial Minds (NAM, human-AI interrelationship). Shared infrastructure includes admin staff, AI Postdoctoral Fellows, research software engineers, data scientists, and a compute cluster with 300 H100 GPUs. Faculty span natural sciences, engineering, social sciences, and humanities.

1140.notes: University of Cambridge flagship mission on AI, led by Neil Lawrence (DeepMind Professor of Machine Learning). Aims to develop AI-enabled innovation that benefits science, citizens, and society by leveraging Cambridge's interdisciplinary research community. Focuses on responsible AI development requiring expertise from research, business, policy, civil society, and affected communities. Engages in public dialogue on AI governance; research has shown public desire for AI systems with meaningful human oversight, transparency, accountability, and protection from harms.

1141.notes: DeepMind Professor of Machine Learning at the University of Cambridge; leads ai@cam, Cambridge's flagship AI mission. Senior AI Fellow at the Alan Turing Institute; visiting professor at University of Sheffield. Previously Director of Machine Learning at Amazon (3 years). Author of 'The Atomic Human: Understanding Ourselves in the Age of AI.' Has given parliamentary testimony on AI regulation. Advocates strongly for public-interest AI governance: warns against regulation that serves 'the digital oligarchy,' cautions about 'sowing the seeds of 10,000 Horizon scandals,' and emphasizes data transparency and privacy. Research focuses on interaction of ML with the physical world, originally motivated by ML deployment in Africa.

1142.notes: Director of Research in Machine Learning at the University of Cambridge; Programme Director for Trust and Society at the Leverhulme Centre for the Future of Intelligence (CFI). MBE. Turing AI Fellow in Trustworthy Machine Learning; heads Safe and Ethical AI at The Alan Turing Institute. Co-director of ELLIS programme on Human-centric Machine Learning. Member of UNESCO Ad Hoc Expert Group on Ethics of AI and IEEE Standards Working Group on Explainable AI. Emphasizes academic engagement with policymakers for 'technically and legally sensible' policy. Research spans interpretability, fairness, privacy, safety, ethics. Has written that humans cannot escape accountability for AI decisions and warns of major labor displacement ahead.

1144.notes: Business school of the University of Oxford. Offers a portfolio of AI-focused executive education programs: Oxford Artificial Intelligence Programme, Oxford Executive Diploma in AI for Business (master's-level), Oxford Leading AI Implementation Programme, and Oxford Generative and Agentic AI Programme (directed by Matthias Holweg and Mike Wooldridge). Draws on Oxford's research in multi-agent architectures, operational design, and responsible AI practice. Focused on business leaders understanding AI workings, possibilities, ethics, and implementation strategy.

1145.notes: Glen de Vries Professor of Health Statistics and professor of Computer Science and Data Science at NYU. Co-head of the Global AI Frontier Lab at NYU with Yann LeCun (2024). PhD from Aalto University (2014). Co-authored the 2014 paper with Bahdanau and Bengio introducing neural machine translation and attention mechanisms. Previously research scientist at Facebook AI Research (2017-2020); co-founded Prescient Design (acquired by Genentech 2021), served as Executive Director of Frontier Research until Jan 2026. Has publicly questioned existential AI risk framing, arguing focus should be on concrete current-application risks (healthcare, military). Critical of alarmist narratives distracting from practical regulation.

1146.notes: Joint U.S.-South Korea AI research center at NYU, established May 2024 via MOU between NYU and Korea's IITP. Co-directed by Yann LeCun and Kyunghyun Cho. Focuses on three pillars: fundamental AI research, trustworthy/responsible AI, and AI for healthcare. Korean government investing ~$34M (45B won) with NYU contributing ~$31M by 2028. Opened September 2024 at One Metrotech Center, Brooklyn.

1147.notes: South Korean government institution under the Ministry of Science and ICT (MSIT) that manages national ICT R&D policy, funding, and human resource development. Key funder of the Global AI Frontier Lab at NYU. Restructured in 2026 around "AI Transformation (AX)" with new teams for Physical AI, AI Foundation (data), and AI Safety and Trust. Manages program areas in AI, AI semiconductors, AX convergence, and physical AI.

1148.notes: Key Senate committee with primary jurisdiction over AI legislation, chaired by Ted Cruz (R-TX) since 2025. Cruz unveiled the SANDBOX Act in September 2025 and a five-pillar AI policy framework explicitly advocating a light-touch, pro-innovation regulatory approach to maintain U.S. leadership over China.

1149.notes: Bipartisan U.S. House task force created in Feb 2024 by Speaker Johnson and Leader Jeffries, co-chaired by Reps. Obernolte and Lieu. Released a 273-page final report in Dec 2024 with 66 findings and 89 recommendations across 15 policy areas. Sunset at end of 118th Congress; no successor body established in the 119th Congress as of early 2025.

1150.notes: U.S. House committee with primary jurisdiction over AI R&D policy, NIST AI standards, and NSF AI programs. Passed nine bipartisan AI bills in Sept 2024 including the CREATE AI Act (NAIRR), NSF AI Education Act, and Workforce for AI Trust Act. Contributed to the Bipartisan AI Task Force report (Dec 2024) with 66 findings and 89 recommendations. Held "Advancing America's AI Action Plan" hearings in 2025-26 with OSTP Director Michael Kratsios. Chaired by Jay Obernolte (R-CA) in 119th Congress.

1151.notes: U.S. House committee with jurisdiction over labor, education, and workforce training policy as it intersects with AI. Launched "Building an AI-Ready America" hearing series in Jan 2026 covering AI in the workplace, workplace safety, AI in education/teaching, and employer-led AI training. Examining AI's impact on hiring, gig work, and worker management. Key forum for workforce displacement and AI readiness policy.

1152.notes: Former U.S. Representative (CA-9, 2007-2023) who chaired the Congressional AI Caucus and authored the AI in Government Act (2020). Transitioned to California State Senate District 5 in Dec 2024. Authored SB 813, which creates the CA Artificial Intelligence Standards and Safety Commission with independent verification organizations for AI safety standards. Previously a Fellow at Stanford HAI and Senior Policy Advisor at Pillsbury Winthrop Shaw Pittman. Also affiliated with the AI Trust Foundation. Math PhD; one of few STEM-trained members of the legislature.

1153.notes: California State Senator (D-Santa Ana, SD-34) and Chair of the Senate Judiciary Committee, which is the key committee for AI regulation bills in the CA legislature. Authored SB 574 restricting lawyers' and arbitrators' use of AI (requiring human review, banning delegation of decisions to AI, protecting confidential data). Publicly opposed Trump executive order to preempt state AI regulation. As Judiciary Chair, shapes which AI bills advance through the legislature, including SB 1047 (Wiener) and other safety bills.

1154.notes: California State Senator (D-San Diego, SD-18) and leading voice on AI consumer protection, especially for minors. Authored SB 243, the nation's first chatbot safeguards law (signed by Newsom), requiring operators to implement safeguards against sexual content for minors and disclosure that chatbots are AI. Also authored SB 420 (California AI Bill of Rights framework), SB 903 (protecting against AI therapy products), and proposed a 4-year moratorium on AI chatbots in children's toys. Publicly criticized OpenAI for lobbying against chatbot protections.

1155.notes: Bureau within U.S. Dept of Commerce responsible for trade promotion and commercial diplomacy. Leads the American AI Exports Program (per Trump July 2025 EO) to promote export of U.S. AI technology stack globally. Establishing National AI Center as gateway for U.S. AI exports. Has a dedicated AI Team in the U.S. Commercial Service. Accepting proposals from industry consortia (April-June 2026) for full-stack AI technology packages to international partners. Led by Under Secretary William Kimmitt.

1156.notes: 17th Under Secretary of Commerce for International Trade, sworn in Aug 2025. Leads ITA and serves as principal trade adviser to Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick. Drives the American AI Exports Program, including leading ITA delegation to India AI Impact Summit 2026 in Bangalore/New Delhi. Previously a partner at Kirkland & Ellis LLP (complex litigation, trade/investment advisory). BA from Duke, JD from Georgetown Law.

1157.notes: U.S. foreign affairs agency with growing AI diplomacy role. Released first Enterprise AI Strategy FY 2024-2025 under Sec. Blinken. Key AI-relevant offices: Office of Special Envoy for Critical and Emerging Technology (S/TECH) for strategic tech competition and allied coordination; Bureau for Cyberspace and Digital Policy (CDP) for AI governance norms. Launched Partnership for Global Inclusivity on AI (PGIAI, Sept 2024) with major AI companies. Deploying internal AI tools including StateChat and agentic AI pilots. March 2026 EO by Newsom is state-level; this is federal.

1158.notes: California state agency (GovOps) overseeing CDT, Office of Data and Innovation, DGS, and CalHR. Leads GenAI implementation across CA state government per Newsom's 2023 executive order. Spearheading CalCompute, a public computing cluster for safe/ethical AI research. Coordinating AI procurement standards under March 2026 EO (N-5-26) requiring vendor trust/safety certifications. Houses the State Technology Council and AI Community of Practice for sharing AI best practices across departments.

1159.notes: California state department under GovOps responsible for procurement, real estate, and administrative services. Key role in AI governance: directed by March 2026 EO (N-5-26) to develop new vendor certification requirements for AI companies seeking state contracts, requiring attestations on content safety (CSAM prevention), bias mitigation, and civil rights/liberties protections. Works with CDT to set AI procurement standards and facilitate employee access to vetted GenAI tools. Recommendations for new AI vendor certifications due within 120 days of EO.

1160.notes: California's central IT agency (CDT), oversees state technology policy and services. Leads AI integration for state government under Gov. Newsom's executive orders (EO N-12-23). Launched 'Poppy,' a GenAI digital assistant for state employees built on large language models, with 20+ state departments testing it. Collaborates with Office of Data and Innovation (ODI) and CalHR on AI workforce training. Implements the state's Acceptable Use of AI policy for government agencies. California enacted 18 AI-related laws in 2024 and signed SB 53 (Transparency in Frontier AI Act) in 2025. In March 2026, Newsom signed a first-of-its-kind executive order strengthening AI protections as the Trump administration rolled back federal guardrails.

1161.notes: CalHR manages California's state workforce (civil service rules, benefits, training). Partnered with the Office of Data and Innovation (ODI) to launch the Foundations of GenAI Certificate series, a free training pathway for state employees to develop responsible GenAI skills. Part of a multi-agency AI workforce effort directed by Gov. Newsom's EO N-12-23, alongside CDT and GovOps. Also involved in creating the Chief Data and AI Officer position at Caltrans. California's Civil Rights Council approved regulations (effective Oct 2025) protecting against AI-related employment discrimination.

1164.notes: New York State's central IT agency (ITS). Authored the state's 'Acceptable Use of Artificial Intelligence Technologies' policy (Jan 2024) under Gov. Hochul's direction, requiring leadership approval for AI systems, human oversight for automated decisions, and NIST AI RMF-based risk assessments. Appointed Eleonore Fournier-Tombs as Chief AI Officer to refine policy. Part of the Empire AI initiative. A 2025 State Comptroller audit found gaps: the policy lacks detailed implementation guidance and ITS has no inventory of AI systems across state agencies. Delivered an AI training tool for the state workforce.

1165.notes: Ohio DJFS administers unemployment insurance, workforce development, and public assistance programs. Won the 2025 Merrill Baumgardner Award from the National Association of State Workforce Agencies for AI innovation in unemployment services.
